description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone block with a roughly rectangular form and uneven, jagged edges. Its surface is predominantly coarse and textured, with visible pitting and natural indentations, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for some time. The coloration is a mix of light gray and off-white, with subtle variations that hint at mineral inclusions or weathering. One side appears slightly flatter, possibly from being in contact with the ground or another surface, while the other faces are more rugged and angular. The stone's mass and size make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside. There are no visible carvings or artificial markings, and the stone appears solid and dense throughout.
description: A large, rough-textured, light gray stone block with irregular, jagged edges and a weathered surface. Its size and mass make it suitable as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: This view shows a slanted, angular face with a pronounced ridge and rough, pitted texture.
description_view_1: This side appears more rectangular and blocky, with a relatively even surface and subtle undulations.
description_view_2: This angle highlights a jagged, broken edge and a coarse, granular surface with visible weathering.
description_view_3: This view reveals a broad, slightly curved face with deep textural variations and a prominent overhang at the top.
attribute_location: urban park boundary or roadside median
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with similar stones to form a barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting with no artificial carvings; natural mineral speckling is visible.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible pitting and minor erosion, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with natural landscaping or other stone features; can be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
